page: Microsoft Azure L4s_v4 is an Lsv4-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 4 vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, it bundles 960 GB of Premium Storage-capable capacity. No GPU resources are included.

Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2158.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression speed of 31.48 GB/sec. However, multi-core scaling is relatively weak, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 3990.37 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 26.74 GB/sec. Memory performance is mixed; while large-block write bandwidth is average at 25.49 GB/sec, read-only memory bandwidth is weak, measuring 36.35 GB/sec uncached. Database operations and static web serving throughput perform in the average tier, with web serving reaching 7.76 GB/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the L4s_v4 offers a favorable qualitative cost-efficiency profile for workloads requiring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial bundled local storage without the overhead of a larger instance. The primary tradeoff is the limited multi-core scaling and lower memory read bandwidth, which may bottleneck highly parallelized in-memory computing. This server is best suited for single-threaded applications, relational databases, text processing, and localized storage-intensive workloads that benefit from the 960 GB local storage and strong single-core execution.
description: Microsoft Azure L4s_v4 is an Lsv4-family virtual machine configured with a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. It features 4 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 960 GB of bundled local storage. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers strong single-core performance, as demonstrated by a Geekbench single-core score of 2158.0 and high single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth are relatively weak. With an 8.0 GB memory-per-core ratio and substantial local storage, the L4s_v4 provides a cost-effective profile for storage-intensive and database workloads that do not require massive parallelization. It is highly suited for relational databases, text processing, and single-threaded applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L4s_v4 is a storage-capable virtual machine featuring 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 960 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L4s_v4 is an Intel-powered VM with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 960 GB local storage, optimized for databases and single-core tasks.
tagline: A storage-bundled virtual machine featuring strong single-core Intel performance and high memory density for database and localized workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 4 vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
32.0 GB of system memory providing a high 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
960 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core performance in text processing and compression
Suited for relational databases, single-threaded tasks, and local storage workloads
